name: La Casella Natural Area – Circular from Alzira via Pla del Barber
name_original: Paraje Natural La Casella — Circular desde Alzira por Pla del Barber
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Alzira traverse a diverse landscape characterized by mountainous terrain, lush valleys, and significant natural parks. The region features the Sierra de Corbera, with peaks like El Caballo Bernat, and the Paraje Natural Municipal de la Murta y la Casella, offering varied elevations and environments. The Júcar River also provides routes with different types of scenery, making it suitable for a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Alzira The most popular mountain bike trail is La Casella Natural Area – Circular from Alzira via Pla del Barber, a 23.1 miles (37.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 49 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses a protected natural park with varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Collado del Infierno Viewpoint – Font del Garrofer loop from Alzira, a moderate 24.7 miles (39.7 km) path. This route offers panoramic views and requires good physical condition. Local mountain bikers also love the Riola (Rio Júcar) – La Raconada Iron Bridge loop from Alzira, a 39.5 miles (63.6 km) trail leading through the Júcar River surroundings, often completed in about 4 hours 0 minutes. Mountain biking around Alzira is defined by mountainous landscapes, natural parks like La Murta y la Casella, and routes along the Júcar River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Alzira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alzira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find around Alzira?
A: Alzira offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from moderate paths through natural parks to more challenging routes with significant ascents in areas like the Sierra de Corbera. The region's landscape includes mountainous terrain, lush valleys, and routes along the Júcar River, providing varied scenery for your rides.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Alzira?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, Alzira does offer options suitable for beginners and families. There are 5 easy mountain bike trails available, providing a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes. These trails typically have less elevation change and are designed for a more relaxed experience.

Q: What are some of the most scenic mountain bike routes in Alzira?
A: Many routes offer stunning views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e908406145/la-casella-natural-area-circular-from-alzira-via-pla-del-barber" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Casella Natural Area – Circular from Alzira via Pla del Barber&lt;/a&gt; route traverses a protected natural park with varied terrain. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21954585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Infierno Viewpoint – Font del Garrofer loop from Alzira&lt;/a&gt;, which provides panoramic views of the surrounding area.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Alzira?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Alzira are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e908406145/la-casella-natural-area-circular-from-alzira-via-pla-del-barber" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Casella Natural Area – Circular from Alzira via Pla del Barber&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39552450" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riola (Rio Júcar) – La Raconada Iron Bridge loop from Alzira&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a longer ride along the Júcar River.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Alzira?
A: The region is rich in natural and historical landmarks. Many routes pass through the &lt;strong&gt;Paraje Natural Municipal de la Murta y la Casella&lt;/strong&gt;, where you can explore the ruins of the 14th-century Monastery of Santa María de la Murta and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4462728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Murta Botanical Trail&lt;/a&gt;. The Sierra de Corbera offers impressive peaks like El Caballo Bernat and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4552325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Ouet&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5608443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de la Valldigna&lt;/a&gt; on some routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Alzira?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Alzira is typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the weather is generally pleasant. Summers can be quite hot, especially in July and August, which might make longer rides more challenging. Winters are usually mild, but occasional rain can affect trail conditions.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Alzira offers 26 difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders seeking a challenge. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31441099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Aigues Vives – La Mediterrania Viewpoint loop from Alzira&lt;/a&gt; cover over 60 km with significant elevation gain, demanding good physical condition and advanced riding skills.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Alzira?
A: The mountain bike trails in Alzira are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 130 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural parks like La Murta y la Casella, and the diverse options available for different ability levels.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Alzira?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always best to check specific regulations for protected areas like the Paraje Natural Municipal de la Murta y la Casella. Generally, dogs are welcome on trails if kept on a leash, but some sensitive ecological zones might have restrictions. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the mountain bike trails in Alzira?
A: Alzira and its surrounding towns offer various options for food and drink. While specific cafes directly on trails might be limited, you'll find restaurants and bars in the villages and towns close to popular trailheads. The area around the Monastery of Aigues Vives, for instance, is often integrated into cycling routes and may have nearby amenities.

Q: How long are the mountain bike trails around Alzira on average?
A: The mountain bike trails around Alzira vary significantly in length. You can find shorter routes, but many popular trails are moderate to long. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e908406145/la-casella-natural-area-circular-from-alzira-via-pla-del-barber" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Casella Natural Area – Circular from Alzira via Pla del Barber&lt;/a&gt; is about 37 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39552450" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riola (Rio Júcar) – La Raconada Iron Bridge loop from Alzira&lt;/a&gt; extends to approximately 63.6 km. This variety allows riders to choose routes that match their desired duration and fitness level.
tips: Beautiful very picturesque place ideal for a bike ride or walk. It can be traveled by paved road from Alzira to the PNM La Casella

Beautiful panoramic point of the Vall de La Casella. It is reached by a forest track in very good condition (PR-CV 303) and it can be seen as far as the city of Alzira. It is a path for mtb or gravel bike, highly recommended, with beautiful views in various sectors of its route.
Very nice track that runs between orange, olive and persimmon trees over the old railway line. Recommended for cycling as it has very little unevenness.

waypoints.name: Natural Trail of the Old Little Train
